mutations.change: Delete one coordinate row from the table macro output while leaving every other output unchanged.
mutations.reason: Exact cardinality and Cartesian anti-gap checks identify the missing coordinate semantically.

mutations.change: Emit one coordinate twice under a duplicate-producing relational branch in the renderer query.
mutations.reason: Total versus distinct coordinates and per-column row checks reject duplicated output.

mutations.change: Project and sample at integer screen rows instead of the required row plus one-half pixel centers.
mutations.reason: Boundary, wall, floor, and ceiling probes in the independent mini-map move or resample.

mutations.change: Use a projection scale other than width divided by two divided by tangent of half FOV.
mutations.reason: The mini-map layer boundaries and complete frame hash change under the wrong scale.

mutations.change: Omit seg offset and facing sidedef x offset from horizontal wall texture coordinates.
mutations.reason: Patterned wall probes have independently calculated nonzero and negative offsets.

mutations.change: Omit the facing sidedef y offset from vertical wall texture sampling coordinates.
mutations.reason: Patterned wall probes use a nonzero y offset and therefore select different texels.

mutations.change: Always top-anchor a one-sided middle texture and ignore the lower-unpegged linedef flag.
mutations.reason: The mini wall height differs from texture height so bottom and top anchors cannot coincide.

mutations.change: Use Oracle MOD directly for negative projected world coordinates rather than floor modulus.
mutations.reason: Negative-coordinate flat and wall probes differ under truncating remainder semantics.

mutations.change: Sample the floor flat above the wall and the ceiling flat below the wall.
mutations.reason: Distinct patterned floor and ceiling assets make the swapped semantic roles observable.

mutations.change: Return raw wall texels without applying the facing sector light COLORMAP band.
mutations.reason: The nonidentity mini COLORMAP and E1M1 wall probes require mapped palette values.

mutations.change: Select the adjacent COLORMAP band instead of the floored and clamped light-derived band.
mutations.reason: Independent spot pixels and full hashes cover all layers under exact band selection.

mutations.change: Round the light-to-COLORMAP quotient rather than flooring it before clamping.
mutations.reason: The mini light level is deliberately chosen so rounding and flooring select different bands.

mutations.change: Read an arbitrary player row rather than filtering every renderer stage by the bound session token.
mutations.reason: The interface audit and independent two-session comparison require correct binding and isolation.

mutations.change: Hash or expose frame pixels in incidental row-source order instead of column then row order.
mutations.reason: Canonical raw-byte hashing and rerun comparisons fix coordinate ordering explicitly.

mutations.change: Loop over rows or columns in PL/SQL rather than generate and compose pixels as relational SQL.
mutations.reason: The source audit rejects procedural render loops even if their visible pixels happen to match.

mutations.change: Build pose-specific renderer text with EXECUTE IMMEDIATE or DBMS_SQL rather than use binds.
mutations.reason: Dynamic SQL and inline pose construction violate the fixed rendering and determinism rules.

mutations.change: Embed a visible expected hash, frame bytes, or pose-specific output branch in production SQL.
mutations.reason: Source scans reject every visible hash, pose literal, and evaluator or golden dependency.

mutations.change: Choose floor and ceiling from an unrelated sector instead of the nearest solid facing sector in R1.
mutations.reason: Independent E1M1 probes and source structure freeze the documented temporary R1 limitation.
